key_hedging_phrases: there can be no guarantee that contracts are not terminated or that contract options will be exercised
our actual results could differ materially from those stated or implied in forward-looking statements
subject to termination provisions, including for convenience
the fluctuation of our results of operations and our key business measures on a quarterly basis in future periods
may exclude all or some portion of the value of certain commercial contracts
key_bullish_phrases: 114% - our Rule of 40 score! These results make undeniable the transformational impact of using AIP to compound AI leverage
Year-over-year growth in our U.S. business surged to 77%, and year-over-year growth in U.S. commercial climbed to 121%
We are yet again announcing the highest sequential quarterly revenue growth guide in our company's history
Closed a record-setting $2.76 billion of total contract value, up 151% year-over-year
U.S. commercial remaining deal value of $3.63 billion, up 199% year-over-year and 30% quarter-over-quarter
key_risk_phrases: our long and unpredictable sales cycle
the impact of recent or future global macroeconomic and geopolitical events, such as the ongoing Russia-Ukraine, and Israel and broader Middle-East conflicts
issues raised by the use of artificial intelligence in our platforms
any breach or access to our or customer or third-party data
the potential or actual imposition of tariffs or other impacts on trade relations
payload.management_tone_summary: Management tone is exceptionally bullish — among the most confident seen in a major-cap tech press release — with CEO language that explicitly frames results as 'undeniable' and 'record-setting' across multiple operating dimensions. The progression from Q2 2025 (Rule of 40 of 94%, U.S. commercial growth of 93% YoY) to Q3 2025 (Rule of 40 of 114%, U.S. commercial growth of 121% YoY) represents a meaningful acceleration in both rhetoric and underlying metrics, with management tone escalating commensurately. The guidance language is highly specific, numeric, and accompanied by upward revisions to every guided metric, signaling strong management conviction in forward momentum.
payload.analyst_revision_rationale: Analysts are likely to meaningfully revise Q4 2025 and full-year 2025 estimates upward across revenue, operating income, and EPS, for several reasons. First, the Q4 revenue guide midpoint of $1.329 billion implies ~61% YoY growth — accelerating from Q3's 63% but setting a new absolute dollar high — and U.S. commercial TCV of $1.31 billion (up 342% YoY) and RDV of $3.63 billion (up 199% YoY) provide unusually strong leading indicators of durable forward revenue. Second, the full-year revenue guidance raise to $4.396–$4.400 billion implies Q4 consensus was likely materially below the new guide, and the U.S. commercial full-year floor of $1.433 billion (104% growth) exceeds prior consensus by a wide margin given prior guidance was 85% growth. Third, adjusted operating margin expansion to 51% in Q3 (vs. 46% in Q2) signals operating leverage is compounding faster than the street models, creating upside risk to adjusted EPS and free cash flow estimates simultaneously. Fourth, the deal count and size metrics (204 deals ≥$1M, 91 ≥$5M, 53 ≥$10M — all materially above Q2's 157/66/42) suggest the customer acquisition and expansion engine is accelerating, not plateauing, which should force analysts to revise future-period customer count and revenue per customer assumptions upward. Finally, management's language is conspicuously free of the usual hedging around macro headwinds or budget delays that would temper upward revisions, further reinforcing the case for broad and large upward estimate revisions.
